name: Additive extrapyramidal symptom risk
actions: Use caution when combining drugs that increase extrapyramidal symptom risk.
Monitor for rigidity, tremor, akathisia, dystonia, parkinsonism, and other movement-related adverse effects (educational).
Consider whether a lower-risk alternative is appropriate if symptoms emerge or risk is high (educational).

rationale: Both drugs increase extrapyramidal symptom risk.
Combined dopamine-blocking effects may increase the likelihood of movement-related adverse effects.

explanation_template: {A_name} and {B_name} both increase extrapyramidal symptom risk. Combined use may increase the likelihood of akathisia, parkinsonism, dystonia, or related movement adverse effects (educational).

references.citation: PD stacking: additive extrapyramidal symptom risk with concurrent dopamine-blocking drugs.
